what_manipulated: whether flanker and target letters match in identity vs. response mapping across modalities
what_measured: reaction time and response accuracy on conflict task
experiment: Two experiments with 36 participants each tested the classical Eriksen flanker effect in a bimodal setting: visual target letters (H or S) with auditory distractor letters. Experiment 1 varied stimulus-onset-asynchrony (0, 250, 500 ms) and measured reaction time and accuracy via ANOVA. Experiment 2 added response-congruent conditions (same response but different letter) to dissociate perceptual from response-level effects. Vincentized RT distributions and conditional accuracy functions analyzed the effect time-course.
